title: Add Graphiti core + MCP server services to compose
userStory: As an operator, I want the Graphiti MCP server running on the docker network so that other services can issue episodes and search calls.
acceptanceCriteria: compose/docker-compose.yml has a graphiti service using zepai/graphiti-mcp:latest (tag verified against upstream MCP repo)

Env vars set: FALKORDB_URI=falkor://falkordb:6379, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (LLM), OPENAI_API_URL=http://ollama:11434/v1 (embedder endpoint — Ollama is OpenAI-compatible), GRAPHITI_GROUP_ID (defaults to 'brain')

compose/graphiti-config.yaml is bind-mounted at /app/config/config.yaml (the upstream default hardcodes gpt-4o-mini + OpenAI; we need our own to pin model + provider). Sets llm.provider=anthropic / model=claude-haiku-4-5 and embedder.provider=openai / model=nomic-embed-text routed through the local ollama service

Sibling 'ollama' service runs in compose alongside graphiti; pulls nomic-embed-text on first start; ~270MB on disk, ~500MB-1GB RAM at runtime. Graphiti depends_on ollama with service_healthy condition
Downstream users swap providers by editing graphiti-config.yaml + .env (OpenAI direct, OpenRouter, local Ollama all documented in .env.example). No code change required
Internal port 8000 reachable on docker network as graphiti:8000; no host port mapping
Verify: docker compose exec graphiti curl -s http://localhost:8000/health returns {"status":"healthy",...}
